nodes.description: Compound heterozygosity for nonsense and missense DSP mutations. Haploinsufficiency alone is tolerated, but combination with a missense allele yields a severe skin phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygosity for non-sense and mis-sense mutations in desmoplakin underlies skin fragility/woolly hair syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Mutation screening of desmoplakin demonstrated compound heterozygosity for a non-sense/mis-sense combination of mutations in both cases, C809X/N287K and Q664X/R2366C, respectively.

nodes.description: Immunohistochemistry of skin biopsies shows desmoplakin protein mislocalized to the cytoplasm rather than its normal cell-peripheral (desmosomal) location.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygosity for non-sense and mis-sense mutations in desmoplakin underlies skin fragility/woolly hair syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Immunohistochemistry of skin biopsies from both affected individuals revealed that desmoplakin was not just located at the cell periphery but there was also cytoplasmic staining.

nodes.description: Loss of functional desmoplakin at the cell periphery causes acantholysis, focal detachment of desmosomes into the intercellular space, and perinuclear condensation of the keratin intermediate filament network — i.e., failure of desmosome–keratin attachment in keratinocytes.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygosity for non-sense and mis-sense mutations in desmoplakin underlies skin fragility/woolly hair syndrome.
evidence.snippet: electron microscopy demonstrated acantholysis throughout all layers of the skin, focal detachment of desmosomes into the intercellular spaces, and perinuclear condensation of the suprabasal keratin intermediate filament network

nodes.description: Focal and diffuse palmoplantar keratoderma with hyperkeratotic plaques extending to trunk and limbs.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygosity for non-sense and mis-sense mutations in desmoplakin underlies skin fragility/woolly hair syndrome.
evidence.snippet: focal and diffuse palmoplantar keratoderma, hyperkeratotic plaques on the trunk and limbs

nodes.description: Skin fragility with non-scarring blistering throughout all layers of the epidermis due to acantholysis.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygosity for non-sense and mis-sense mutations in desmoplakin underlies skin fragility/woolly hair syndrome.
evidence.snippet: electron microscopy demonstrated acantholysis throughout all layers of the skin, focal detachment of desmosomes into the intercellular spaces, and perinuclear condensation of the suprabasal keratin intermediate filament network
